Main_Figures.title: Integrated evidence architecture and study design
Main_Figures.core_question: How can a population DEHP oxidative-metabolism signal be connected conservatively to CGM meal vulnerability without overclaiming causality?
Main_Figures.recommended_panels: A. Graphical evidence cascade; B. Dataset flow and sample sizes; C. MSI bridge phenotype construction; D. Claim hierarchy and no-overclaim guardrails; E. Analysis module timeline.
Main_Figures.current_status: New design needed; previous schematic can be rebuilt as cleaner data-annotated graphical abstract.
Main_Figures.source_data: Table1_dataset_and_phenotype_summary.csv; phase0 registry; claim guardrails; module manifest
Main_Figures.key_message: This is a staged bridge-phenotype design: DEHP oxidative profile -> MSI in NHANES; MSI -> PPGR vulnerability in CGMacros.
Main_Figures.journal_role: Front-door figure for Nature/Cell/Lancet-style readers; explains why the cross-dataset design is valid but cautious.
Main_Figures.risk_if_missing: Reviewers may misread the work as a direct DEHP-to-CGM causal claim or as disconnected datasets.
Main_Figures.needed_work: Rebuild as high-end graphical abstract with minimal text, dataset sizes, and a clear 'not direct causality' design note.

Main_Figures.title: DEHP oxidative-metabolism profile is associated with metabolic susceptibility in NHANES
Main_Figures.core_question: Is the environmental exposure-profile layer statistically robust?
Main_Figures.recommended_panels: A. Survey-weighted forest plot for primary and secondary exposures; B. Weighted quartile dose-response; C. spline curve; D. MSI-version robustness; E. sensitivity/negative-control panel; F. subgroup or effect-modification heatmap.
Main_Figures.current_status: Partly implemented in Figure1_NHANES_exposure_response_publication_v2; should be expanded for top-tier submission.
Main_Figures.key_message: The DEHP oxidative profile is consistently associated with MSI and insulin-resistance/glycemic markers.
Main_Figures.risk_if_missing: The environmental component appears underpowered or merely decorative.
Main_Figures.needed_work: Add robustness strip and subgroup heatmap; keep main forest/dose/spline as central panels.

Main_Figures.title: Construction, robustness, and transfer of the metabolic susceptibility phenotype
Main_Figures.core_question: Is MSI a stable bridge phenotype rather than an arbitrary score?
Main_Figures.recommended_panels: A. MSI component z-score architecture; B. PCA/component weights; C. MSI-version correlation matrix; D. NHANES vs CGMacros MSI distributions; E. component profiles across MSI tertiles; F. missingness/availability waterfall.
Main_Figures.current_status: Not yet built as a main figure; data available.
Main_Figures.key_message: MSI behaves consistently across construction variants and can bridge NHANES and CGMacros.
Main_Figures.journal_role: Defends the bridge variable, which is the manuscript's central methodological hinge.
Main_Figures.risk_if_missing: Reviewers may attack MSI as post hoc or unstable.
Main_Figures.needed_work: Generate as a polished multi-panel figure before any top-tier submission.

Main_Figures.title: MSI identifies free-living postprandial glycemic vulnerability in CGMacros
Main_Figures.core_question: Does the bridge phenotype manifest as dynamic meal-level vulnerability?
Main_Figures.recommended_panels: A. Meal-level iAUC distribution by MSI tertile; B. peak distribution; C. subject-level MSI-response gradient; D. cluster-robust model coefficient forest; E. bootstrap estimates; F. leave-one-subject influence.
Main_Figures.key_message: Higher MSI is a robust main-effect marker of PPGR vulnerability.
Main_Figures.risk_if_missing: The CGM evidence looks like summary-only association rather than true meal-level biology.
Main_Figures.needed_work: Add bootstrap and leave-one-subject influence as E-F or Extended Data if main figure becomes dense.

Main_Figures.title: Person-by-food-matrix susceptibility refines carbohydrate-only interpretation
Main_Figures.core_question: Why is the biology not just 'more carbs, more glucose'?
Main_Figures.recommended_panels: A. Digestibility-risk vs iAUC meal scatter; B. iAUC and peak model forest; C. MSI x digestibility predicted response surface; D. high-risk food-matrix feature enrichment; E. counterfactual meal redesign effect; F. candidate high-risk meal matrix examples if image permissions allow.
Main_Figures.current_status: Partly implemented in Figure3_FoodMatrix_CurvePhenotypes_publication_v2; response surface and counterfactual panel not yet built.
Main_Figures.key_message: High-MSI individuals appear especially vulnerable to high-digestibility-risk food matrices.
Main_Figures.risk_if_missing: The manuscript may look like another CGM prediction paper rather than a nutrition-mechanism paper.
Main_Figures.needed_work: Add predicted interaction surface and counterfactual redesign panel; keep proxy caveat in legend.

Main_Figures.title: CGM curve phenotypes reveal prolonged and delayed response vulnerability
Main_Figures.core_question: Does MSI/food-matrix susceptibility affect response shape, not only scalar iAUC/peak?
Main_Figures.recommended_panels: A. Cluster map by iAUC/peak/time-to-peak/recovery; B. cluster composition by MSI tertile; C. delayed-peak/prolonged-elevation model forest; D. prototype response curves or reconstructed curves; E. MSI-digestibility to curve-phenotype alluvial plot.
Main_Figures.current_status: Partly implemented inside Figure3 v2; should become independent figure if targeting a top-tier journal.
Main_Figures.key_message: Susceptibility is expressed as dynamic CGM response phenotypes, especially large/prolonged responses.
Main_Figures.journal_role: Adds physiologic depth and makes CGM dynamics visible.
Main_Figures.risk_if_missing: Dynamic CGM data are underused; reviewers may see only static iAUC/peak outcomes.
Main_Figures.needed_work: Generate prototype curve panel if raw time-series alignment is feasible; otherwise use feature-derived clusters and model forest.

Main_Figures.title: MSI-enhanced models improve personalized high-response stratification
Main_Figures.core_question: Does the biology improve practical meal-risk prediction?
Main_Figures.recommended_panels: A. observed vs predicted iAUC; B. observed vs predicted peak; C. nested model AUC/MAE/R2; D. calibration deciles; E. conformal interval coverage/width; F. decision-curve net benefit; G. performance by MSI tertile.
Main_Figures.key_message: MSI adds incremental predictive value beyond macro/pre-CGM context models.
Main_Figures.risk_if_missing: Prediction claims may look unsupported or not TRIPOD/PROBAST-aligned.
Main_Figures.needed_work: Add conformal interval and decision-curve panels; present prediction as research stratification, not clinical tool.

Main_Figures.title: External CGM datasets define transportability boundaries
Main_Figures.core_question: Where does the framework generalize, and where does it not?
Main_Figures.recommended_panels: A. response-scale comparison across datasets; B. response ratio vs CGMacros; C. T1D macro directionality; D. Stanford same-food heterogeneity; E. Jaeb healthy-adult time-above-threshold reference; F. transportability evidence map.
Main_Figures.source_data: external_transportability_map.csv; external_domain_shift_metrics.csv; phase5 external validation tables.
Main_Figures.key_message: External data support heterogeneity and macro-response directionality while clarifying non-replication boundaries.
Main_Figures.journal_role: Preempts overgeneralization and frames boundary conditions honestly.
Main_Figures.risk_if_missing: Reviewers may demand external validation or criticize generalizability.
Main_Figures.needed_work: Add Stanford same-food heterogeneity and Jaeb healthy reference if space allows.

Main_Figures.title: Mechanistic plausibility map linking DEHP oxidative metabolism, MSI, and PPGR vulnerability
Main_Figures.core_question: Is there a biologically plausible pathway, even without wet-lab data?
Main_Figures.recommended_panels: A. curated mechanism knowledge graph; B. pathway enrichment bar plot; C. literature evidence-map matrix; D. current evidence vs missing causal tests.
Main_Figures.current_status: Not yet built as a main figure; data available from Module E and literature module.
Main_Figures.key_message: The integrated observational/CGM signal is biologically plausible but not mechanistically proven.
Main_Figures.journal_role: Useful for discussion or graphical abstract; may be Extended Data depending on journal length.
Main_Figures.risk_if_missing: Mechanistic novelty may feel thin for top-tier translational journals.
Main_Figures.needed_work: Keep conservative; do not make causal arrows beyond evidence level.

Main_Tables.title: Datasets, phenotypes, and analytic roles
Main_Tables.columns: Dataset; role; participants; meals/events/readings; MSI available; key exposure/outcome; main limitation
Main_Tables.journal_role: One-page transparent overview of all evidence layers.

Main_Tables.title: Primary NHANES exposure-susceptibility estimates
Main_Tables.columns: Exposure; outcome; beta; 95% CI; P; q; model/covariates; analytic n
Main_Tables.journal_role: Main epidemiology results table, complements Figure 2.

Main_Tables.title: Primary CGMacros MSI-PPGR and food-matrix models
Main_Tables.columns: Outcome; model block; term; estimate; 95% CI; q; meals; participants; interpretation
Main_Tables.journal_role: Compact table for main CGM and food-matrix estimates.

Main_Tables.title: Prediction performance and incremental value of MSI
Main_Tables.columns: Target; model; MAE; R2; AUC; AUPRC; calibration slope; net benefit; delta vs M2
Main_Tables.journal_role: Prediction/translational table aligned with TRIPOD+AI expectations.

Main_Tables.title: Claim-level evidence strength and guardrails
Main_Tables.columns: Claim; supporting figure/table; dataset; inference type; causal status; limitation; required wording
Main_Tables.journal_role: May be main or supplement; extremely useful for top-tier cautious framing.

Claims_Crosswalk.claim: DEHP oxidative-metabolism profile is associated with metabolic susceptibility in NHANES.
Claims_Crosswalk.allowed_wording: associated with; consistent across sensitivity analyses
Claims_Crosswalk.avoid_wording: causes; mediates; predicts CGM response directly

Claims_Crosswalk.claim: MSI is a robust bridge phenotype across construction variants.
MSI identifies higher free-living meal-level PPGR vulnerability in CGMacros.
High-digestibility-risk food matrices amplify MSI-related PPGR vulnerability.
CGM curve phenotypes reveal delayed/prolonged vulnerability beyond iAUC and peak.
Claims_Crosswalk.evidence_type: Feature engineering / unsupervised or rule-based dynamic phenotype

Claims_Crosswalk.claim: MSI improves high-response meal-risk stratification within CGMacros.
External CGM datasets define boundary conditions rather than direct replication.
Claims_Crosswalk.evidence_type: External boundary and domain shift evidence
Claims_Crosswalk.allowed_wording: boundary evidence; supports heterogeneity and directionality

Claims_Crosswalk.claim: Mechanistic literature supports biological plausibility but not causal proof.
Claims_Crosswalk.evidence_type: Curated knowledge graph and literature map

Reviewer_Strategy.textual_response_strategy: Bridge-phenotype design shown explicitly; no direct DEHP-to-CGM claim.
Show component construction, PCA weights, version correlations, no-BMI sensitivity.
Include weighted covariate table, sensitivity, negative-control-like analyses, composition models.
Show meal-level n=1498, subject-level gradient, bootstrap and leave-one-subject influence.
Explicitly label as dry-lab proxy; provide codebook and feature distributions.
Show leakage audit and predictor dictionary.
Frame external data as boundary evidence; avoid deployment claims.
Mechanism graph and pathway enrichment, conservative language.
Separate primary, secondary, exploratory and boundary claims.
Put nutrition mechanism and CGM precision nutrition at center, exposure as upstream susceptibility context.
